summary: The erdos_problems dataset contains 1,217 problems, of which the available issue-level detail covers only erdos_0001 and erdos_0002 — both fully resolved with proof issues closed. The 'Proof Status' section shows ten quarterly buckets (q1–q10) each holding exactly 100 issues, suggesting uniform batching rather than organic, problem-specific activity. Concrete per-problem traction cannot be reliably inferred beyond the two documented entries due to limited granularity in the provided context.
problems: erdos_0001: 1 resolved issue (proof completed), 0 open — fully closed
erdos_0002: 1 resolved issue (proof completed), 0 open — fully closed
Proof Status buckets q1 through q10 each report exactly 100 issues, an unnaturally uniform distribution that likely reflects pagination or batch assignment rather than true activity
Only 2 of 1,217 problems (0.16%) have any documented issue detail, leaving the activity profile of the remaining ~1,215 problems unknown
highlights: Both documented problems (erdos_0001, erdos_0002) are fully resolved with proofs verified and no open issues
No backlog of open issues exists in the detailed problem records, indicating clean closure workflow
Consistent proof-issue naming convention ('Proof of erdos_XXXX') supports easy automated tracking and reporting
Even q1–q10 distribution implies a structured, manageable workload pipeline if the buckets are actionable units

suggested_todos.title: Expand per-problem issue reporting beyond erdos_0001/0002 to surface true activity and traction across all 1,217 problems
Investigate why q1–q10 each contain exactly 100 issues — confirm whether this is real data or a pagination/batching artifact
Cross-reference solved/disproved status flags with cash-prize and OEIS tags to identify high-value problems near resolution
Build a 'likely solvable soon' shortlist by filtering open problems with recent issue activity and partial-proof annotations
Archive fully resolved problems (e.g., erdos_0001, erdos_0002) into a closed set to focus team attention on open work
